title: the fate of Yui Gendo and Fuyutsuki
display_title: the fate of Yui Gendo and Fuyutsuki
wikitext:
[]: the fate of Yui, Gendo and Fuyutsuki             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue May 03, 2011 7:50 pm Everybody remember the scene after "but what will you do, mother?", the flashback conversation by the tree? Yeah? What if it isn't a flashback? I'd like to propose the notion that what we're seeing is actually a direct response from within the instrumentality-like experience of being in 01's core. Yui is going off to the stars but is bringing company; Fuyutsuki and chibi-Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue May 03, 2011 7:54 pm Like all the theories that say Yui had company, this is contradicted by the fact that her closing voiceover says she's going alone. I've never understood what part of "alone" is unclear.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue May 03, 2011 8:01 pm Only if you assume the flashback isn't a flashback, so NemZ could be.... Never mind.

[]: Re: the fate of Yui, Gendo and Fuyutsuki             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Tue May 03, 2011 8:04 pm ...

[]: Re: the fate of Yui, Gendo and Fuyutsuki             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue May 03, 2011 8:05 pm NemZ wrote: Everybody remember the scene after "but what will you do, mother?", the flashback conversation by the tree? Yeah? What if it isn't a flashback? I'd like to propose the notion that what we're seeing is actually a direct response from within the instrumentality-like experience of being in 01's core. Yui is going off to the stars but is bringing company; Fuyutsuki and chibi-Gendo.

[]: Re: the fate of Yui, Gendo and Fuyutsuki             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue May 03, 2011 9:06 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: and chibi-Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 04, 2011 7:41 am This is so completely made up... and would sorta not really sit well with the message.... To infer so much from a few little details whereas the most intuitive way to see the Dialogue would say she's alone is just too much.

[]: Re: the fate of Yui, Gendo and Fuyutsuki             Quote Post by flapjack » Wed May 04, 2011 8:34 am bump

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed May 04, 2011 2:09 pm My post disappeared, so let me repeat. In that conversation, the dialogue says "for that person" or something like it. One person. Not two dozen or whatever.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed May 04, 2011 2:24 pm The object may be a bit vague in that line. Reichu's translation from the commentary makes it seem like "it" might be referring to Eva-01 and not the person's soul inside. Yui says: "Even after 5 billion years, when the Earth, the Moon, and the Sun are gone, [he/she/it] will remain. If [he/she/it] alone can go on living, by [him/her/itself]… It will be very lonely, but as long as [he/she/it] exists…" Bochan Bird translated the object to "it" and Reichu chose to use "she" (Evas = Females, etc...). Not saying that I think there's a party in Eva-01's core, but the dialogue in question could be interpreted as such.

[]: Re: the fate of Yui, Gendo and Fuyutsuki             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Wed May 04, 2011 2:42 pm What incentive would Yui exactly have for doing this? I get the impression she was pretty upset with the way Gendo treated Shinji, and I don't think she loved Fuyutski the way he loved her. I think she would have preferred for both of them to return to the normal world.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed May 04, 2011 3:20 pm rhfxz1s wrote: What incentive would Yui exactly have for doing this?

[]:             Quote Post by flapjack » Thu May 05, 2011 4:47 am bump

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu May 05, 2011 5:48 am Because he didn't read ahead in the script? I'm not claiming Fuyu is psychic.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu May 05, 2011 6:02 am flapjack wrote: So, if fuyu is going to be talking to Yui at some point post 3I, why does he ask Gendo to "Say hello to Yui for me"? That's my only qualm with this hypothesis.

[]:             Quote Post by flapjack » Fri May 06, 2011 6:52 am bump

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri May 06, 2011 8:05 am You're mad, NemZ. Mad I say! Clearly my faith in you has not been misplaced. It's a crazy idea, as you say, but it's also very cool in its way. It also makes Yui's departure seem a little less batshit insane (granted, just about anything would, because that pretty much tops the insanity meter...). I like it.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri May 06, 2011 8:36 pm Bagheera wrote: You're mad, NemZ. Mad I say!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri May 06, 2011 8:40 pm I'm not sure I take it seriously, but, as you surely know, I'm generally in favor of tenaciously interpreting around the whole "Yui does nothing but float around the abyss of space in complete solitude until the Big Crunch" thing.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Fri May 20, 2011 11:14 pm I wonder why Fuyutsuki was in that last scene anyways. I mean, they're all together in Instrumentality, but he's not that important of a character. But he, along with the rest of the Nerv Staff, are in the original 25 and 26. Let me go check what sort of important advice he had to give to Shinji then.
